rawCommits.subject: feat(interface): suppress native browser context menu
rawCommits.body:
Right-clicking empty AURA chrome was showing the WebView2 / Chromium
native menu (Back, Refresh, Save as, Print, More tools, Inspect),
breaking the desktop OS feel. Install a single document-level listener
that defers to any in-app onContextMenu (DesktopContextMenu,
NotesEntryContextMenu, ProcessCanvas, ...) via event.defaultPrevented,
and otherwise cancels the native menu. Inside editable text fields
(input, textarea, contenteditable) we replace the native menu with a
compact in-app Cut / Copy / Paste / Select All so right-click editing
still works without giving up control of the chrome.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): widen top-edge resize hit area on Windows
rawCommits.body:
The borderless main window inherits tao's default WM_NCHITTEST
behavior, which only reports HTTOP within SM_CYFRAME (~4 logical
px) of the top edge. Combined with the floating titlebar pill
that begins 5 px below the window edge, hitting the resize zone
required pixel-perfect aim.

Install a Win32 subclass on the main HWND that returns
HTTOP/HTTOPLEFT/HTTOPRIGHT for any cursor inside a 10-logical-px
band along the top, falling through to DefSubclassProc for every
other position so titlebar drag, side resize, and Chromium
app-region all keep working unchanged. The subclass is dispatched
before tao's by virtue of being installed last, becomes a no-op
while the window is maximized, and re-reads DPI on each hit-test
so it stays consistent across mixed-DPI monitor setups.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: widen top-edge resize hit area on Windows

rawCommits.subject: feat(interface): defer-open pattern for async-loading modals
rawCommits.body:
When the user clicked Delete on an agent attached to multiple
projects, the confirm modal opened immediately, then janked: the
"Delete" button widened to "Delete and remove from N projects" and
a cascade-warning paragraph appeared once bindings finished loading.
Same flicker existed for the marketplace Hire modal (in-modal
spinner -> project list) and the Tier subscription modal ("Loading
plan details..." -> tier grid).

Introduce useDeferredModalOpen, a tiny shared hook that runs the
caller's prepare() (e.g. cascade.refresh / projectsStore.refresh /
billingStore.fetchSubscription), keeps the modal closed and shows a
disabled trigger button until the prepare resolves, and only then
flips isOpen true. A 3000ms failsafe opens the modal even if prepare
never settles. Stale resolutions from a previous open cycle are
ignored via an internal cycle token.

Apply to three call sites:
- DeleteAgentConfirmModal (AgentList + AgentInfoPanel) - also shorten
  the cascade label from "Delete and remove from N projects" to
  "Delete (N projects)" so width is stable across binding counts.
- HireProjectPickerModal (MarketplaceMainPanel) - drop the in-modal
  spinner; the per-card Hire button disables briefly while preparing.
- TierSubscriptionModal (OrgSettingsPanel via OrgSettingsRewards /
  OrgSettingsBilling) - read subscription from billing-store instead
  of fetching on open; the Upgrade / Change Plan trigger disables
  briefly while preparing.

The remaining modals from the audit (AgentSelectorModal,
BuyCreditsModal, ProjectSettingsModal, etc.) are deliberately left
for follow-up PRs - the shared hook is the substrate for those.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: feat: split AURA into dev and stable build channels
rawCommits.body:
Introduce a build-time channel selector on aura-os-core
(`stable-channel` default, `dev-channel` opt-in via cargo features)
so a `cargo run`-built dev AURA can run side-by-side with the
installed stable AURA on one machine without colliding on data
dirs, ports, single-instance locks, skills trees, or window
identity. Remote services stay shared via .env.

The new `Channel` enum in `aura-os-core::channel` is the single
source of truth for every per-channel identifier: data dir name
(`aura` vs `aura-dev`), skills home (`~/.aura` vs `~/.aura-dev`),
Windows single-instance mutex, window/menu title, embedded
desktop server port (19847 vs 19848), standalone server port
(3100 vs 3101), harness sidecar port (19080 vs 19081), default
harness URL port (8080 vs 8081), and updater enablement (off in
dev so a cargo-built shell can never silently replace itself
with the stable installer).

`cargo run -p aura-os-desktop` and the `scripts/dev/*` runners
default to the dev channel via cargo's default feature. The
release pipeline (`cargo packager`, `verify-desktop.mjs`, the
`release-stable` and `release-nightly` workflows) builds with
`--no-default-features --features stable-channel`. The desktop
crate disables default features on its `aura-os-core` and
`aura-os-server` deps so a stable packaging build cannot
accidentally pull in `dev-channel` from a transitive default.

Dev script defaults bumped to dev-channel ports (Vite 5174,
mobile backend 3101) so a developer running `npm run dev` or the
mobile runner does not bind on top of an installed stable AURA.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: split AURA into dev and stable build channels

rawCommits.subject: feat(interface): re-enable 3D chat mode with image-first flow
rawCommits.body:
Bring back the 3D pill in the chat mode selector and rewire it so
the only valid path is "generate an image first, then convert it to
3D" -- exactly how the standalone AURA 3D app works. Manual image
attachments for chat 3D mode are disabled because the proxy decode-
and-forward route is broken; the legacy code path is left in place
but commented out so flipping it back on is trivial once the proxy
is fixed.

The input bar now derives its 3D source from the most recent
successful generate_image tool result in the thread, surfaces it
as a "Source for 3D" thumbnail above the textarea, blocks Send with
a "switch to Image mode" hint until one exists, and dispatches the
3D request via generate3dStream's URL form. The resolved-send
pipeline carries sourceImageUrl through to both the live and
queue-replay paths.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: re-enable 3D chat mode with image-first flow

rawCommits.subject: fix(aura3d): stop appending style-lock suffix to image prompts
rawCommits.body:
The AURA 3D app's Image tab was the only call site appending the
product-photography STYLE_LOCK_SUFFIX to user prompts. Send the prompt
verbatim instead so image generation behaves consistently with chat
/image and /3d. The constant is retained so `stripStyleLock` can still
clean the suffix off legacy artifacts persisted before this change.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stop appending style-lock suffix to image prompts

rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): recognize renamed CEO via system-prompt prefix
rawCommits.body:
After renaming the bootstrap CEO (e.g. "CEO" -> "Maia") and re-logging
in, the server was creating a duplicate "CEO" agent. `looks_like_ceo`
only recognised renamed CEOs via the locally-stamped
`bootstrap:ceo_agent_id` setting or an exact name+role both equal to
"CEO". CEOs created before commit 2dabef61a (the stamping fix) never
had the local stamp, so a post-rename login fell through both checks
and `dedupe_ceo_agents` returned no canonical, causing
`setup_ceo_agent` to mint a fresh duplicate.

Add a third identity signal: the canonical CEO `system_prompt` prefix
("You are the CEO SuperAgent"), extracted into a shared constant so
the matcher and the template stay in lock-step. The agent editor
preserves `system_prompt` on save unless the user explicitly rewrites
it, so this prefix survives a rename and lets the dedupe step pick the
older renamed CEO as canonical and best-effort delete the freshly-
minted duplicate on the next setup call.

Adds four unit tests: renamed-name-only, full rename, arbitrary system
prompt is not a CEO, and a drift guard that `ceo_system_prompt()`
always starts with `CEO_SYSTEM_PROMPT_PREFIX`.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: recognize renamed CEO via system-prompt prefix

rawCommits.subject: feat(interface): copy menu for selected non-editable text
rawCommits.body:
After turning off the native browser context menu, right-clicking a
text selection in chat / LLM output (which renders as static markdown,
not a contenteditable) just suppressed the menu and showed nothing.
Detect a non-collapsed window.getSelection() that covers the click
target and surface a Copy-only menu in that case. Editable fields,
app-claimed events, and plain non-editable empty areas keep their
existing behavior unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: copy menu for selected non-editable text

rawCommits.subject: Rework chat 3D mode as in-bar two-step image-then-3D pipeline
rawCommits.body:
- Adds STYLE_LOCK_SUFFIX to the chat 3D image step only; image mode and
  the AURA 3D app remain verbatim.
- Replaces the "Generate an image first" gate with a removable pinned
  source thumb owned by chat-ui-store; one-shot seeding from thread
  history preserves the cross-mode shortcut.
- Splits the 3D wire variant into image_step / model_step branches in
  resolve-send + useChatStream + useAgentChatStream.
- Relocates STYLE_LOCK_SUFFIX to interface/src/constants/generation.ts.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Rework chat 3D mode as in-bar two-step image-then-3D pipeline

rawCommits.subject: Refresh stale 3D-mode doc comments
rawCommits.body:
Updates two leftover code comments that still described the old
"must generate an image first, then switch to 3D" gate (replaced in
2b78e87 by the in-bar two-step pipeline). No behavior change.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(ci): drop unsupported cargo build flags from cargo packager call
rawCommits.body:
cargo-packager 0.11.8's CLI does not accept --no-default-features or
--features; those are cargo-build flags. The stable-channel feature is
already wired in via `before-packaging-command` in
apps/aura-os-desktop/Cargo.toml, so the packager applies it correctly
during the build step it runs internally. Passing the flags to
`cargo packager` itself was failing CI with `unexpected argument`.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: drop unsupported cargo build flags from cargo packager call

rendered.title: Native desktop polish, dual build channels, and a rebuilt 3D chat flow
rendered.intro: Tonight's nightly leans heavily into making AURA feel like a real desktop app: the browser context menu is gone, the Windows top edge is finally grabbable, and async-loading modals stop flickering open. Underneath, AURA now ships as parallel dev and stable channels so contributors can run a cargo build alongside their installed app, and the chat 3D mode returns with a redesigned image-then-model pipeline.

entries.title: Right-click chrome, Windows resize edge, and modal open jank
entries.summary: A trio of desktop polish fixes: AURA reclaims right-click from the WebView, makes the Windows top edge actually grabbable, and stops confirm modals from resizing themselves on open.

items.text: Right-clicking AURA chrome no longer surfaces the WebView2/Chromium native menu (Back, Refresh, Save as, Inspect…). A document-level override defers to in-app context menus when present, suppresses the native one elsewhere, and replaces it inside text fields with a compact Cut / Copy / Paste / Select All.

On Windows, the borderless main window now reports HTTOP/HTTOPLEFT/HTTOPRIGHT across a 10-logical-px band along the top edge via a Win32 WM_NCHITTEST subclass, replacing the ~4px default that made resizing alongside the floating titlebar pill require pixel-perfect aim. The subclass is DPI-aware and disables itself while maximized.

Async-loading confirm modals no longer pop open and then visibly reflow. A new useDeferredModalOpen hook waits for prepare() to resolve (with a 3s failsafe) before flipping the modal open, applied to the Delete Agent confirm, the marketplace Hire picker, and the Tier subscription modal. The cascade label was also shortened to “Delete (N projects)” for stable width.

entries.title: Dev and stable channels can coexist on one machine
entries.summary: AURA now compiles into two distinct channels — stable (installed) and dev (cargo run) — that no longer collide on data dirs, ports, single-instance locks, or the auto-updater.

items.text: A new Channel enum in aura-os-core is the single source of truth for per-channel identity: data dir (aura vs aura-dev), skills home (~/.aura vs ~/.aura-dev), Windows single-instance mutex, window/menu title, embedded server (19847/19848), standalone server (3100/3101), harness sidecar (19080/19081), default harness URL port, and updater enablement (off in dev so a cargo build can never replace the installed stable app).

cargo run and the scripts/dev/* runners default to dev-channel via cargo's default feature, while the release-stable, release-nightly, and desktop-validate workflows build with --no-default-features --features stable-channel. Vite (5174) and the mobile backend (3101) also moved to dev ports so npm run dev no longer binds on top of an installed AURA.

entries.title: Chat 3D mode returns with an image-first source thumbnail
entries.summary: The 3D pill is back in the chat mode selector, wired through a generate-image-then-convert flow that mirrors the standalone AURA 3D app, and a stray style-lock suffix in the 3D app's Image tab is removed.

items.text: Chat 3D mode is re-enabled and now derives its source from the most recent successful generate_image tool result in the thread, surfaced as a “Source for 3D” thumbnail above the textarea. Send is blocked with a switch-to-Image hint until a source exists, and manual attachments are intentionally hidden while the proxy decode-and-forward path is broken.

The AURA 3D app's Image tab no longer silently appends the product-photography STYLE_LOCK_SUFFIX to user prompts, so image generation there now matches /image and /3d behavior. The constant is retained so stripStyleLock can still clean legacy artifacts.

entries.title: Renamed CEO agents survive re-login without duplication
entries.summary: Logging in after renaming the bootstrap CEO no longer mints a duplicate “CEO” agent for users whose CEO predates the local stamping fix.

items.text: looks_like_ceo gained a third identity signal — the canonical CEO system_prompt prefix (“You are the CEO SuperAgent”) — extracted into a shared constant so the matcher and template stay in lock-step. Combined with the editor preserving system_prompt across renames, dedupe_ceo_agents can now pick the renamed CEO as canonical and remove the freshly-minted duplicate.

entries.title: In-bar two-step 3D pipeline and a Copy menu for chat selections
entries.summary: The 3D chat flow gets a second pass — a removable pinned source thumb replaces the gate — and the new context-menu override learns to handle selected text in non-editable surfaces like chat messages.

items.text: Right-clicking selected text in chat output and other static markdown now surfaces a Copy-only menu instead of doing nothing. The override detects a non-collapsed selection that contains the click target; editable fields, app-claimed events, and empty non-editable areas keep their existing behavior.

Chat 3D mode is reworked into an in-bar two-step pipeline: the “generate an image first” gate is replaced by a removable pinned source thumb owned by chat-ui-store, with one-shot seeding from thread history. The 3D wire variant splits into image_step / model_step branches across resolve-send, useChatStream, and useAgentChatStream, and STYLE_LOCK_SUFFIX is scoped to the chat 3D image step only. Stale doc comments describing the old gate were also refreshed.

entries.title: Unblock CI packaging after the channel split
entries.summary: A follow-up to the dev/stable channel split removes cargo-build flags from the cargo packager invocation that was failing CI.

items.text: cargo-packager 0.11.8 doesn't accept --no-default-features or --features on its own CLI, which broke desktop-validate and both release workflows with “unexpected argument”. The flags are dropped from the packager call and the stable-channel feature is now applied via before-packaging-command in apps/aura-os-desktop/Cargo.toml, which cargo-packager runs internally.

highlights: Native browser context menu replaced by in-app Cut/Copy/Paste and Copy-on-selection menus
Windows top edge resize widened from ~4px to a reliable 10px band
Dev and stable build channels can now run side-by-side on one machine
Chat 3D mode returns as a two-step image-then-3D pipeline with a pinned source thumb
